description: Japanese people who were the first generation of immigrants from Japan
description_wiki: Issei (一世, "first generation") are Japanese immigrants to countries in North America and South America.

Sansei (三世, "third generation") is a Japanese and North American English term used in parts of the world (mainly in South America and North America) to refer to the children of children born to ethnically Japanese emigrants (Issei) in a new country of residence, outside of Japan.

description: group of historically related denominations of Protestant Christianity

aliases: The town of Our Lady the Queen of the Angels of the Little Portion
description: seat of Los Angeles County, and largest city in California, United States
description_wiki: Los Angeles (often referred to by its initials, LA) is the most populous city in the U.S. state of California, and the commercial, financial, and cultural center of Southern California.

description: capital city of Hiroshima Prefecture, Japan
description_wiki: Hiroshima (広島市, Hiroshima-shi; English: , also UK: , US: , Japanese: [çiɾoɕima] ) is the capital of Hiroshima Prefecture in Japan.

description: city in and county seat of King County, State of Washington, United States
description_wiki: Seattle (  see-AT-əl) is the most populous city in the U.S. state of Washington and the Pacific Northwest region of North America.

description: consolidated city and county in California, United States
description_wiki: San Francisco, officially the City and County of San Francisco, is the fourth-most populous city in California and the 17th-most populous in the United States, with a population of 826,079 in 2025.

description: public research university in Seattle, Washington, United States
description_wiki: The University of Washington (UW, and informally U-Dub or U Dub) is a public research university in Seattle, Washington, United States.

description: Buddhist temple in Kyoto, Japan. Head temple of Honganji-ha school
description_wiki: Nishi Hongan-ji (西本願寺) is a Buddhist temple in Shimogyō-ku, Kyoto, Japan.

description: capital city of the U.S. state of California and seat of Sacramento County
description_wiki: Sacramento is the capital city of the U.S. state of California.

description: county seat of Fresno County, California, United States
description_wiki: Fresno ( ; Spanish for 'ash tree') is a city in the San Joaquin Valley of California, United States.

aliases: Pueblo de San José de Guadalupe
Pueblo de San Jose de Guadalupe
description: city and county seat of Santa Clara County, California, United States
description_wiki: San Jose, officially the City of San José (  SAN hoh-ZAY, -⁠SAY; Spanish: [saŋ xoˈse]), is the most populous city in the San Francisco Bay Area and Northern California, and the 12th-most populous in the United States, with 997,368 residents.

description: state of the United States of America
description_wiki: Minnesota is a state in the Upper Midwestern region of the United States.

surface_forms: World War I broke out in 1914
the year after it was over
description_wiki: World War I, or the First World War (28 July 1914 – 11 November 1918), also known as The Great War, was a global conflict between two coalitions: the Allies (or Entente) and the Central Powers.

surface_forms: the twenty-seventh year of the Meiji period
the eleventh year of the Meiji period
description: Japanese era from October 1868 to July 1912

description_wiki: Buddhism, also known as Buddha Dharma and Dharmavinaya, is an Indian religion and philosophy based on teachings attributed to the Buddha, a śramaṇa and religious teacher who lived in the 6th or 5th century BCE. It is the world's fourth-largest religion, with about 320 million followers, known as Buddhists, who comprise 4.1% of the global population.
Broadway High School, originally known as Seattle High School, opened in Seattle, Washington, in 1902 and was the first dedicated high school built in Seattle.

description: public high school in Seattle, USA
description_wiki:
Franklin High School is a public high school in Seattle, Washington,
located in its Mount Baker neighborhood and administered by Seattle Public Schools.
